text: The company is AI-native when its operating model changes: fewer people coordinate less, agents execute more of the repeat work, and humans focus on direction, taste, relationships, validation, and accountability.

evidence.quote: The company is AI-native when its operating model changes: fewer people coordinate less, agents execute more of the repeat work, and humans focus on direction, taste, relationships, validation, and accountability.

text: The fundamental change is how fast the company can learn, iterate, and evolve, and the second company will outlearn the first every single day.

evidence.quote: The fundamental change is how fast the company can learn, iterate, and evolve.

text: A diagnostic to ascertain the state of the context layer: pick one representative task, hand it to a fresh agent with only the workspace context, and ask for three next actions; two or more strong suggestions means the context layer is carrying weight; three generic answers means the context is thin.

evidence.quote: pick one representative task, hand it to a fresh agent with only the workspace context, and ask for three next actions.

evidence.quote: A call transcript is raw.

text: Guardrails belong in code and config, not in prompts; the non-negotiables are explicit: cost caps per run and per day, retry caps, a maximum tool-call depth, scoped credentials per agent identity, no production writes without an approval step, no auto-merge on code, and a fleet-wide kill switch.

evidence.quote: Guardrails belong in code and config, not in prompts.

text: A skill is reusable instructions plus examples for a recurring task; run it by hand twice, then encode the parts that repeat.

evidence.quote: A skill is reusable instructions plus examples for a recurring task.

text: An eval has three layers: hand-labeled ground truth, deterministic checks, and an LLM judge for what the deterministic checks can't reach.

evidence.quote: An eval has three layers, and they stack in order.

text: Below roughly 70% acceptance rate—minor edits still count as accepted—the skill isn't ready to move up an autonomy level; when acceptance is low, rewriting the prompt is almost never the fix; it's usually loading more context at runtime, tightening the skill's scope, adding more worked examples, or writing a clearer escalation rule.

evidence.quote: Below roughly 70% — minor edits still count as accepted — the skill isn't ready to move up an autonomy level to run with less oversight than it has now.
